name: SOFT-TISSUE CHONDROMA
definition: EXTRASKELETAL CHONDROMA = CHONDROMA OF SOFT PARTS rare benign cartilage-forming tumor in extraarticular soft tissue consisting of small nodule of benign hyaline cartilage tumor akin to enchondroma

demographics.Histo: lobules of adult-type hyaline cartilage with areas of calcification + ossification; myxoid

dominant_finding: typically punctate / curvilinear / ringlike chondroid calcifications (in 33-70%)
discriminating_features: lobulated well-defined extraskeletal mass < 2 cm in size
may contain calcifications (33-70%) with ringlike appearance / ossifications
scalloping of adjacent bone with sclerotic reaction
typically punctate / curvilinear / ringlike chondroid calcifications (in 33-70%)

general: lobulated well-defined extraskeletal mass < 2 cm in size
may contain calcifications (33-70%) with ringlike appearance / ossifications
scalloping of adjacent bone with sclerotic reaction
CT: typically punctate / curvilinear / ringlike chondroid calcifications (in 33-70%)
MR: multilobulated mass of high signal intensity on T2WI
low to intermediate SI relative to muscle on T1WI
radiolucent lesion with variable degrees of chondroid matrix calcifications
cortical excavation of tissue forming shallow shelving depression = saucerization ← local
round mass with hyperintense T2 signal ← high water content of chondroid matrix
